handle.title: Username
handle.description: Your username is your public name across the app — change it here and it updates everywhere.
handle.prefixHint: 3–18 characters. Letters, numbers, and hyphens only.
handle.customHint: To use your own domain, add a DNS TXT record at _atproto pointing to your account ID, then save.

errors.prefixChars: Use only lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens. It can’t start or end with a hyphen.
errors.full: Enter a full username like alice.example.com.
errors.generic: Could not update your username. Please try again.
errors.unavailable: Changing your username isn’t available yet. Please try again later.

agentKeys.title: AI agent keys
agentKeys.description: Give an AI agent its own key so it can add records on your behalf — without ever sharing your password.
agentKeys.hint: Keys can add and edit your stewardship records, but can never change your account settings or manage other keys. Revoking a key cuts the agent off instantly.
agentKeys.freshHint: Your key is shown once and is already in the prompt above — paste it into your agent now. Manage or revoke keys below.
agentKeys.revokeConfirm: Revoke “{name}”? Any agent using this key loses access immediately.

errors.copy: Copy failed — select the text manually.
errors.nameRequired: Name the key after the agent it's for.

agentKeys.connectCaption: Paste this into your AI agent (ChatGPT, Claude, a Telegram bot…) to connect it to GainForest.

docs.metaDescription: The open AT Protocol record schemas behind GainForest — biodiversity observations, impact claims, funding, and identity.
docs.title: Data schemas
docs.lead: The open record formats behind GainForest. Every observation, project, certificate, donation, and post is stored as a structured record using the schemas below.

sections.feed: Social primitives ported from Bluesky into the GainForest commons — posts (and reply comments) and likes, so the feed can be posted to, commented on, and liked.
sections.dwc: Biodiversity occurrence records aligned with the TDWG Simple Darwin Core standard. Occurrences reference a shared event; measurements reference an occurrence.
sections.ac: Media and passive-acoustic-monitoring records aligned with TDWG Audiovisual Core. Audio and multimedia link to a Darwin Core occurrence as evidence.
sections.certified: Identity primitives from the Hypercerts ecosystem — actor profiles, organizations, follows, badges, and locations.
sections.hypercerts: Impact claims (activity, contribution, contributor, rights), collections, funding receipts, attachments, work scope, and shared types.
